blog.title: Claude Code v2.1.215 Verify and Code Review Workflow

long.title: Why Polished AI Outputs Still Need Careful Review

results.content:
Claude Code stopped auto-running /verify and /code-review. Use this explicit finish gate. #ClaudeCode
https://indieseek.co/blogs/claude-code-verify-code-review-explicit-workflow/?utm_source=x&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-20&utm_content=blog

Claude Code 2.1.215 stopped invoking /verify and /code-review automatically, which can silently remove a review step from an established coding-agent workflow.

This guide rebuilds the finish gate with deterministic checks, explicit skill calls, a scoped review target, and a compact evidence block.

It also separates local review from managed GitHub Code Review so teams do not treat AI output as the merge decision.

Claude Code no longer invokes /verify and /code-review for you. This guide shows how to rebuild the finish gate with deterministic tests, explicit skill calls, scoped review, and evidence your team can inspect.
https://indieseek.co/blogs/claude-code-verify-code-review-explicit-workflow/?utm_source=facebook&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-20&utm_content=blog

Claude Code stopped auto-running /verify and /code-review. Rebuild the finish gate with deterministic tests, explicit review scope, and handoff evidence. #ClaudeCode #AIEngineering
https://indieseek.co/blogs/claude-code-verify-code-review-explicit-workflow/?utm_source=mastodon&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=daily_content_2026-07-20&utm_content=blog

Claude Code stopped invoking /verify and /code-review automatically. This guide rebuilds the finish gate with tests, explicit review scope, and auditable evidence.

results.content:
Not every task needs an agent. This 55-second test shows when complex judgment, brittle rules, or messy data make one worth it. #AIAgents
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b2RTwP-pVCo

Not every workflow becomes better when you add an agent. This Tiny Agent short gives a practical test: use agents where judgment is complex, rules are brittle, or unstructured data dominates—and keep deterministic software where it is enough.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b2RTwP-pVCo

Not every task needs an agent. In 55 seconds, this video shows the three conditions that make an agent useful—and the simpler option to choose when those conditions are missing.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b2RTwP-pVCo

Not every task needs an agent. Use one when judgment is complex, rules are brittle, or data is messy; keep deterministic software when it is enough. #AIAgents #Automation
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b2RTwP-pVCo

Not every task needs an agent. This short shows when complex judgment, brittle rules, or messy data justify one—and when deterministic software wins.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=b2RTwP-pVCo

results.content:
A polished AI draft can still hide bad assumptions. Review Goal, Gaps, Grounds, Test, and Owner before release. #AIReview
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ayBWGV-ocDA

Polish is not proof. A fluent AI draft can hide weak assumptions, missing evidence, and unowned decisions. This video turns review into a reusable five-pass loop: Goal, Gaps, Grounds, Test, and Owner—so the first draft becomes an inspectable result.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ayBWGV-ocDA

A polished AI answer can feel finished before anyone checks its assumptions or evidence. This video gives you a five-pass review loop for turning that first draft into a decision you can actually defend and own.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ayBWGV-ocDA

Polished AI output is not finished work. Review Goal, Gaps, Grounds, Test, and Owner before release. #AIReview #HumanInTheLoop
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ayBWGV-ocDA

A polished AI draft can still hide weak assumptions and missing evidence. Use this five-pass review loop: Goal, Gaps, Grounds, Test, and Owner.
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ayBWGV-ocDA
